task: I’m drafting a reply to a vendor about a test failure, and the explanation they sent doesn’t seem to line up with the evidence. Can you help me write a short, professional note that asks for the missing details and keeps the discussion focused on what can actually be verified?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short, professional version you can use:

Hi [Name],

Thanks for the update. The explanation doesn’t yet align with the evidence we have. Please provide the specific data or observations supporting your conclusion, and clarify whether this is a test method issue, a product issue, or something else. We need to keep the discussion tied to verifiable facts so we can determine the next action.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it firmer for escalation or shorter for a quick email reply.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but a little too generic. I want it tighter and more pointed — something that asks for the exact missing details without sounding like I’m inviting a debate.

Something like:

Hi [Name],

The explanation you sent doesn’t match the failure data we have. Please send the specific evidence supporting your conclusion — test conditions, measurements, sample IDs, and any changes to method or product configuration — so we can compare it against the recorded results. Until we have that, I’m keeping the discussion limited to verifiable facts.

Best,
[Your Name]

That’s the level I need. Short, clean, no fluff.

turns.agent_response:
Got it — here’s a tighter version in that style:

Hi [Name],

The explanation you sent doesn’t match the failure data we have. Please send the specific evidence supporting your conclusion — test conditions, measurements, sample IDs, and any method or product configuration changes — so we can compare it against the recorded results. Until then, let’s keep this limited to verifiable facts.

Best,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it even firmer or strip it down further.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Tight enough, and it stays on the evidence instead of turning into a back-and-forth.
